rootCauses.vitestTimeout: src/test/work-detail-metadata.test.ts imported the full work detail page server-component tree only to test metadata generation; the import timed out under full threaded suite contention.
rootCauses.docker8080Failure: Windows IP Helper owned 0.0.0.0:8080 through a stale portproxy rule forwarding to the WSL IP, so Docker Desktop could not publish backend 127.0.0.1:8080.

fixes: Extracted buildWorkDetailMetadata into a deterministic helper used by generateMetadata and tested directly.
Added scripts/dev-up.sh preflight diagnostics for occupied Windows-side backend publish ports.
Validated Playwright against an intentionally started compose stack with BACKEND_PUBLISH_PORT=18080.

validations.command: PLAYWRIGHT_EXTERNAL_SERVER=1 PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3000 npm run test:e2e -- tests/admin-work-video-create-flow.spec.ts tests/admin-work-video-edit-flow.spec.ts tests/admin-work-video-drag-order.spec.ts tests/admin-work-video-s3-compatible.spec.ts tests/public-work-videos.spec.ts

greenCondition: Green using BACKEND_PUBLISH_PORT=18080 ./scripts/dev-up.sh and PLAYWRIGHT_EXTERNAL_SERVER=1 PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3000
remainingRisk: Default backend publish port 8080 remains blocked on this machine until the stale Windows portproxy is removed with elevated permissions.
